results.possibleIdentity: Charles R. Belensky was an engineer at Grumman Aerospace (formerly Grumman Aircraft Engineering Corp.) based in Bethpage, New York, specifically associated with Plant 5. He served as the Chief Biomedical Instrumentation Engineer during the Apollo program's Lunar Module (LM) development in the late 1960s and early 1970s. In this capacity, he was responsible for designing and managing biomedical monitoring systems used during ground testing of the Lunar Module's environmental control system. His work involved close collaboration with astronauts and physicians to ensure safety during high-risk developmental testing within vacuum chambers. Belensky's technical expertise spanned electronics and biomedical engineering, and he was active in professional circles such as the Range Commanders Council Telemetry Group through the mid-1980s.
